mnemonics: Memoryless: Only the Present Matters. Markov Chains: Making Yesterday Irrelevant, Connecting Now to Next.
keyPoints: A Markov Chain is a stochastic model describing a sequence of events where the probability of each event depends only on the state of the previous event.
The system is 'memoryless' (Markov Property); future states depend only on the current state, not on the sequence that preceded it.
It is defined by a set of possible states and a transition matrix, which contains the probabilities of moving from one state to another.
commonMistakes: Assuming past states beyond the immediate previous one influence the next state, violating the Markov property.
Confusing the transition probabilities in the matrix (e.g., misreading P(i,j) as the probability from j to i instead of from i to j).
